programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Toxic Space Factories"
programme.desc: Will it ever make sense to use outer space to offshore human pollution and waste?

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Canberra"
programme.desc: Canberra is the youngest city to feature in our series; a mere “adolescent” in our rapidly urbanizing world. It stands out for all the right reasons as it affords those who live within its low-density realm a very privileged lifestyle indeed.

programme.title: Science of Sound S1 - "Sticky Lyrics"
programme.desc: Have you ever gotten a song stuck in your head? Marty Music sat down with Dr. Carole Scherling, a neuroscientist from Belmont University, to talk all about sticky lyrics and the science behind why some catchy tunes get stuck in our heads.

programme.title: Science of Sound S1 - "Power of Nostalgia"
programme.desc: Music has the power to transport us back in time. Marty Music sat down with Nicole Richard, a neurologic music therapist, and professor from Belmont University to talk about how music affects our brains and stirs up memories.

programme.title: Science of Sound S1 - "Imagination Soundtrack"
programme.desc: Professional athletes use music to get psyched up for a big game. But why does music have the power to inspire, motivate, and make us cry? Marty Music finds out how music makes us move.

programme.title: Science of Sound S1 - "Live Music Vibes"
programme.desc: It’s easy to love live music. Whether you’re jamming with some friends or you’re lost in the crowd at an amazing show, live music really moves us. But why does it feel so much better than listening to music on the best home stereo?

programme.title: Suppressed Science S1 - "Human Enhancement"
programme.desc: From a cutting-edge, science-based gym & spa, to a medical clinic that offers direct ultrasound brain stimulation designed to increase mental performance.

programme.title: Suppressed Science S1 - "Genetic Editing"
programme.desc: The discovery of CRISPR Cas-9 in 2012 opened up seemingly limitless potential in the field of genetic editing.

programme.title: Suppressed Science S1 - "Alternative Energy Sources"
programme.desc: In this installment, Athena decodes some of the difficulties faced by the scientists & environmentalists looking to fundamentally change our relationship with energy.

programme.title: Suppressed Science S1 - "Theories Of Everything"
programme.desc: Over the centuries, scientific theories have emerged that rival religious cosmology in terms of ambition and scope. But can science ever truly explain everything?

programme.title: Digits S1 - "Connecting to the Future"
programme.desc: Muller escorts viewers to the physical heart of the Internet, revealing the very first message sent by UCLA professor Leonard Kleinrock, and demonstrating how Internet co-creators Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn devised a common protocol language.

programme.title: Digits S1 - "Man vs. Machine"
programme.desc: Get a sneak peek into how the Internet of Things will soon dominate daily life. As Edward Snowden provocatively points out, “TV is a medium that you watch. The Internet is a medium that watches you as you watch it.”

programme.title: Digits S1 - "Guardians of the Web"
programme.desc: Explore how our reliance on the web creates vulnerability to everything from cyber theft to an Internet system failure. Muller introduces viewers to “white hat” hackers, the U.S. Secret Service’s cybercrime division and even West Point soldiers.

programme.title: DRONERS S1 - "Guerilla Droners"
programme.desc: From New York to Hong Kong, DRONERS tags along with FPV crews to cover underground races and meet the key-players in the emerging drone community.

programme.title: DRONERS S1 - "Kid Droners"
programme.desc: DRONERS goes behind-the-scenes at an international racing tournament to discover the young pilots climbing the ranks - and raking in the cash along the way.

programme.title: DRONERS S1 - "Freestyle Droners"
programme.desc: DRONERS races cars, flies up mountains, and explores abandoned buildings in a rush of adventure to find out what it really takes to be a freestyle pilot.

programme.title: DRONERS S1 - "Next-Gen Droners"
programme.desc: DRONERS discovers how the technological advances driven by the FPV community are pushing the envelope of what’s possible in this fast-moving community

programme.title: Power & S2 - "Denis Boivin"
programme.desc: Canadian engineer Denis Boivin has invented the track n Go, which makes it possible for any 4x4 vehicle to become a snowmobile in 15 minutes.

programme.title: Power & S2 - "Oluwaseyi Sosanya"
programme.desc: Gravity Sketch is an intuitive multi-platform 3D creation tool developed by Oluwaseyi Sosanya and two other students of the Royal College of Art.

programme.title: Power & S2 - "Rob McCall"
programme.desc: Rob McCall has built a futuristic catamaran super yacht that looks like something that would transport a James Bond villain to a secret location.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "52 Blocks - Tear Coleman"
programme.desc: Tear hails from New York City and is a 52 blocks master, a defensive fighting style invented by African Americans in prisons. Instead of hitting, you block.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Music"
programme.desc: We use music to nurture our children, comfort the sick, and worship the unseen. Music is inherently a human act, but today, artificial intelligence and algorithms have gotten more and more involved in music production, distribution, and marketing.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Communication"
programme.desc: From the first written word to the Internet, we’ve continued to innovate how we communicate with each other. What happens when technology advances to the point that ideas and thoughts can be shared instantly?

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Transportation"
programme.desc: From empires to major metropolitan cities, transportation has been crucial. As cities grow more congested and overwhelmed with toxic air, how will future transportation help get us where we need to be?

programme.title: Rethink S1 - "The Leidenfrost Effect"
programme.desc: In a world where nothing is what it seems, let’s play games with colour changing milk. Learn about chroma keys in video editing and do some mental math gymnastics. We’ll also explore the Leidenfrost effect and take a look at rethinker Linwood G Dunn.

programme.title: Rethink S1 - "Rotoscoping"
programme.desc: In a world where nothing is what it seems, we’ll discuss the animation technique of Rotoscoping, take another look at card tricks, the history of juggling and the popularity of the Smart phone.

programme.title: Rethink S1 - "Tilt Shift Photography and Night Hag Syndrome"
programme.sub-title: Tilt Shift Photography and Night Hag Syndrome
programme.desc: In a world where nothing is what it seems, we’ll introduce you to Tilt Shift Photography, Night Hag syndrome and tightrope walking. Plus explore this weeks featured rethinker and tightrope walker Maria Spelterini.

programme.title: Rethink S1 - "The Van de Graaff Generator"
programme.desc: In a world where nothing is what it seems, let’s take a look at the depth perception of 3D visuals and discover the Van de Graaff generator. Have you heard of overtone singing, we’ll explain this and more in Rethink.

programme.title: The Aviators S2 - "Dream flight of a lifetime"
programme.desc: Twenty pilots attend a T33 Silverstar ground school but only one will win a chance to actually fly it. We visit the Joe Nall event, the “Oshkosh” of RC flying, and Raeleen goes flying with rock stars of the air show circuit, the Aeroshell Aerobatic Team.

programme.title: The Aviators S2 - "Aircraft ownership"
programme.desc: Kurtis explores the varied costs and entry points of flying for everyone from the weekend warrior to the rich and famous. When your driveway is a runway, life is good… and as airparks go, this place is Heavenly! Are two engines really better than one?

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Fire and Water"
programme.desc: Host/adventurer George Kourounis follows the fabled route of the Klondike Gold Rush sourdoughs through the bawdy town of Skagway, Alaska, paddling the white water rivers and the White Horse Rapids into the Yukon.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "The Winds of Autumn"
programme.desc: The autumn of 2006 brings hurricanes to the North Atlantic and hot dry winds fan forest fires across Northern Ontario. Host/adventurer George Kourounis has traveled to some awesome hurricanes, including Rita and Katrina, with his pal Mark Robinson.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Wild Weather of 2005"
programme.desc: Host/adventurer George Kourounis tracks twisters through the Tornado Alley of Oklahoma and Texas, descends into the Erte Ale Volcano in the Danakil Depression of Ethiopia, and then witnesses Hurricane Katrina destroying the town of Gulfport.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Helping Hands"
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive with dozens of bull sharks in Fiji. We then head to South Florida to plant native mangroves in an area of need. The journey ends with a trip to Maui.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "The ringside doctor"
programme.desc: Get to know about the challenges of a doctor who accompanies and treats boxers during their fights.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Alternative medicine in Turkey"
programme.desc: Check the traditional and alternative medical treatments in Turkey.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "On the way to a new racing age"
programme.sub-title: On the way to a new racing age
programme.desc: Get a glimpse at the ultimate all-electric race car that breaks records on the most famous race tracks in the world.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Innovative Eurocup Draw, Electric Vehicles & Digital Security"
programme.sub-title: Innovative Eurocup Draw, Electric Vehicles & Digital Security
programme.desc: Catch Hamburg’s innovative Euro 2024 draw, a device boosting transporters in winter, and insights from a digital security fair.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S2 - "Terry's Reindeer Game"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry test a scene from Reindeer Games when Ben Affleck and Charlize Theron get trapped under a frozen lake, and they escape by shooting 3 shots with a 12-gauge shot gun underwater to blast a hole in the ice to escape.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S2 - "Ex-Men The Origins of Terry"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry test a famous scene from X-men. They are attempting to see if you can split a bullet with a Katana sword

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S2 - "Pay Terry Back"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry test 2 scenes from Mel Gibson’s film Payback. Mel's character is in the back of a taxi when 4 bad guys pull up next to him in an SUV, they open fire and Mel uses a bad guy in the cab as a shield.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S1 - "Sleep"
programme.desc: Jack meets two people who can’t stop falling asleep and finds out the consequences of sleep deprivation

programme.title: The Demolition Man S1 - "Paris"
programme.desc: Two high-rises in Meaux have to be demolished without endangering the surrounding people and buildings.

programme.title: The Demolition Man S1 - "Belgium"
programme.desc: In this episode a 170 meter tower is imploded! The structure is close to a public highway so there’s an extra element of concern for local safety.

programme.title: The Demolition Man S1 - "The Best of Part 1"
programme.desc: Brendan Moar is here to uncover the people and machinery behind the worlds most dangerous and captivating demolitions.

programme.title: Extremes S1 - "Tall Buildings, Fast Animals and Expensive Cities"
programme.sub-title: Tall Buildings, Fast Animals and Expensive Cities
programme.desc: In this episode of Extremes, we explore tall buildings, fast animals, expensive cities and extremely impressive basketball records.

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Living Virtually"
programme.desc: How did the invention of photography lead us to a world where we live entirely virtually? Alie Ward will point us towards a future where we might upload ourselves to the web.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "WIFI"
programme.desc: Actress Hedy Lamarr, “the world's most beautiful woman,” had a knack for inventing. During WWII, in an effort to help the Allies, she devised a technology designed to intercept Axis communications – and created the foundation of wireless internet.
